left-right-join.label: LEFT and RIGHT JOIN
left-right-join.summary: Include unmatched rows with LEFT and RIGHT JOIN.

sketch0.title: What are LEFT and RIGHT JOINs?

sketch1.title: When to use LEFT or RIGHT JOIN?

sketch-1.title: What are LEFT and RIGHT JOINs?
sketch-1.bodyMarkdown: A **LEFT JOIN** returns all rows from the left table and the matched rows from the right table. If there is no match, the result is NULL on the right side. A **RIGHT JOIN** does the opposite: it returns all rows from the right table and the matched rows from the left table, with NULLs for unmatched left rows. These joins are useful when you want to include all records from one table, even if there is no corresponding record in the other.

sketch-2.title: When to use LEFT or RIGHT JOIN?
sketch-2.bodyMarkdown: Use a **LEFT JOIN** when you want to keep all records from the first (left) table, regardless of whether they have matches in the second (right) table. Use a **RIGHT JOIN** when you want to keep all records from the second (right) table, regardless of matches in the first (left) table. This is helpful for finding unmatched records or ensuring no data is lost from a particular table.

quiz-1.title: Understanding LEFT JOIN
quiz-1.prompt: Which statement best describes what a LEFT JOIN does?
quiz-1.hint: Think about which table's rows are always included.

help.concept: A LEFT JOIN includes all rows from the left table, even if there is no match in the right table.
help.hint_1: LEFT JOIN keeps all rows from the first table and adds data from the second table if available.
help.hint_2: If a row in the left table has no match in the right table, it still appears in the result with NULLs for the right table columns.

options.a: Returns only rows that have matches in both tables.
options.b: Returns all rows from the left table and matching rows from the right table.
options.c: Returns all rows from the right table and matching rows from the left table.
options.d: Returns only unmatched rows from both tables.

quiz-2.title: Choosing the Correct JOIN
quiz-2.prompt: You want to list all products, including those that have never been ordered. Which JOIN should you use between the products and orders tables?
quiz-2.hint: Which JOIN keeps all rows from the products table?

help.concept: A LEFT JOIN keeps all rows from the left table, even if there are no matches in the right table.
help.hint_1: You want to keep all products, even those with no orders.
help.hint_2: LEFT JOIN is used when you want to keep all rows from the first table regardless of matches.

quiz-3.title: Identifying Results of RIGHT JOIN
quiz-3.prompt: Which of the following are true about RIGHT JOIN? (Select all that apply)
quiz-3.hint: Think about which table's rows are always present and what happens to unmatched rows.

help.concept: RIGHT JOIN always includes all rows from the right table, with NULLs for unmatched left table columns.
help.hint_1: RIGHT JOIN keeps all right table rows, even if there is no match in the left table.
help.hint_2: If a right table row has no match in the left table, the left columns will be NULL.

options.a: All rows from the right table are included in the result.
options.b: Rows from the left table are included only if there is a match.
options.c: Unmatched rows from the right table will have NULLs for left table columns.
options.d: It is the same as INNER JOIN.

quiz-4.title: Order the JOIN Types by Inclusion
quiz-4.prompt: Arrange these JOIN types from most exclusive (fewest rows) to most inclusive (most rows) when joining two tables.
quiz-4.hint: Which JOIN type only includes matches? Which includes all from both tables?

help.concept: INNER JOIN returns only matching rows, while OUTER JOINs include unmatched rows from one or both tables.
help.hint_1: INNER JOIN is the most restrictive; FULL OUTER JOIN is the most inclusive.
help.hint_2: LEFT and RIGHT JOINs are in between, each including all rows from one table.

quiz-5.title: Fill in the JOIN Type
quiz-5.prompt: To include all employees, even those who have not completed any training, you should use a _____ JOIN between employees and trainings.
quiz-5.hint: Which JOIN keeps all rows from the first table?

help.concept: LEFT JOIN keeps all rows from the left table, regardless of matches in the right table.
help.hint_1: You want to keep all employees, even if they have no trainings.
help.hint_2: LEFT JOIN is used to include all rows from the first table.

quiz-6.title: Write a LEFT JOIN Query
quiz-6.prompt: Write a SQL query to list all products and their inventory quantities, including products that have no inventory record. Use the products_catalog dataset.
quiz-6.hint: Use LEFT JOIN to include all products. Join on the product_id column.

help.concept: LEFT JOIN includes all rows from the left table and matching rows from the right table. Use ON to specify the join condition.
help.hint_1: Start with SELECT and FROM products. Use LEFT JOIN inventory ON product_id.
help.hint_2: Make sure to select columns from both products and inventory, and join on product_id.

quiz-6.starterCode:
SELECT ... FROM products p
LEFT JOIN inventory i ON ...;
